content:
# Numbers Chapter 2 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios pp. 147–148; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 111–112 (LXX) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_002_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_002_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_002_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_002_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

**2** "The sons of Israel shall encamp each man by his own standard, with the ensigns of the houses of their fathers; at a distance around the tent of meeting they shall encamp.

**3** And those who encamp on the east side toward the sunrise shall be of the standard of the camp of Judah, by their hosts; and the leader of the sons of Judah shall be Nahshon son of Amminadab.

**4** And his host and their numbered ones: seventy-four thousand and six hundred.

**5** And those who encamp beside him shall be the tribe of Issachar; and the leader of the sons of Issachar shall be Nethanel son of Zuar.

**6** And his host and their numbered ones: fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

**7** The tribe of Zebulun; and the leader of the sons of Zebulun shall be Eliab son of Helon.

**8** And his host and their numbered ones: fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

**9** All the numbered ones of the camp of Judah: one hundred and eighty-six thousand and four hundred, by their hosts. They shall set out first.

**10** The standard of the camp of Reuben shall be on the south side, by their hosts; and the leader of the sons of Reuben shall be Elizur son of Shedeur.

**11** And his host and their numbered ones: forty-six thousand and five hundred.

**12** And those who encamp beside him shall be the tribe of Simeon; and the leader of the sons of Simeon shall be Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.

**13** And his host and their numbered ones: fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.

**14** And the tribe of Gad; and the leader of the sons of Gad shall be Eliasaph son of Reuel.

**15** And his host and their numbered ones: forty-five thousand and six hundred and fifty.

**16** All the numbered ones of the camp of Reuben: one hundred and fifty-one thousand and four hundred and fifty, by their hosts. And they shall set out second.

**17** And the tent of meeting shall set out, the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camps; as they encamp, so shall they set out, each man in his place, by their standards.

**18** The standard of the camp of Ephraim, by their hosts, shall be on the west side; and the leader of the sons of Ephraim shall be Elishama son of Ammihud.

**19** And his host and their numbered ones: forty thousand and five hundred.

**20** And beside him shall be the tribe of Manasseh; and the leader of the sons of Manasseh shall be Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.

**21** And his host and their numbered ones: thirty-two thousand and two hundred.

**22** And the tribe of Benjamin; and the leader of the sons of Benjamin shall be Abidan son of Gideoni.

**23** And his host and their numbered ones: thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

**24** All the numbered ones of the camp of Ephraim: one hundred and eight thousand and one hundred, by their hosts. And they shall set out third.

**25** The standard of the camp of Dan shall be on the north side, by their hosts; and the leader of the sons of Dan shall be Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.

**26** And his host and their numbered ones: sixty-two thousand and seven hundred.

**27** And those who encamp beside him shall be the tribe of Asher; and the leader of the sons of Asher shall be Pagiel son of Ochran.

**28** And his host and their numbered ones: forty-one thousand and five hundred.

**29** And the tribe of Naphtali; and the leader of the sons of Naphtali shall be Ahira son of Enan.

**30** And his host and their numbered ones: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

**31** All the numbered ones of the camp of Dan: one hundred and fifty-seven thousand and six hundred. They shall set out last, by their standards."

**32** These are the numbered ones of the sons of Israel by the houses of their fathers. All the numbered ones of the camps, by their hosts: six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

**33** But the Levites were not numbered among the sons of Israel, as YHWH had commanded Moses.

**34** And the sons of Israel did according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses; so they encamped by their standards, and so they set out, each man by his clan, by the house of his fathers.

---

## Translation Notes

**Verse 2 — "standard" (דֶּגֶל / degel):** The Hebrew דֶּגֶל denotes a tribal banner or standard around which divisions rallied. The LXX (Vaticanus) renders this with τάγμα ("division/order"), interpreting the organizational function rather than the physical object. The formal rendering retains "standard" to preserve the concrete Hebrew image.

**Verse 2 — "ensigns" (אֹתֹת / otot):** A distinct term from דֶּגֶל, referring to the identifying signs or emblems of the ancestral houses within each tribal standard. The LXX uses σημεία ("signs"). Rendered "ensigns" to distinguish it from "standard" while preserving the martial register.

**Verse 2 — "at a distance" (מִנֶּגֶד / minneged):** Literally "from opposite" or "at a distance opposite." The LXX reads ἐναντίοι ("facing/opposite"). The Hebrew conveys both spatial separation and orientation toward the tent of meeting. "At a distance around" captures both senses.

**Verse 3 — "east side toward the sunrise" (קֵדְמָה מִזְרָחָה / qedmah mizrachah):** A double expression: קֵדְמָה ("eastward") and מִזְרָחָה ("toward the sunrise"). The MT uses both terms for emphasis; the LXX has πρὸς ἀνατολάς ("toward the east"). The formal rendering preserves the Hebrew doublet.

**Verse 14 — "Reuel" vs. "Deuel":** The MT reads רְעוּאֵל (Reuel) here in Num 2:14, while Num 1:14 has דְּעוּאֵל (Deuel). The Leningrad Codex confirms resh (ר) in this verse. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads Ραγουηλ (Raguel = Reuel). The variation between ד (dalet) and ר (resh) is a well-known scribal issue due to their graphic similarity. This translation follows the MT of each respective verse.

**Verse 17 — "the tent of meeting shall set out":** The Hebrew וְנָסַע אֹהֶל־מוֹעֵד places the tent of meeting as the subject of the verb "set out" (נסע), treating the dismantled tabernacle as a marching unit carried by the Levites. The LXX similarly has ἐξαρεῖ ἡ σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("the tent of witness shall be lifted up").

**Verse 17 — "each man in his place" (אִישׁ עַל־יָדוֹ / ish al-yado):** Literally "each man upon his hand/side," an idiom meaning each in his appointed position. The LXX renders ἕκαστος ἐχόμενος ("each holding to [his position]").

**Verse 32 — Grand total: 603,550:** This matches the total from the census in Num 1:46, confirming internal consistency: Judah camp (186,400) + Reuben camp (151,450) + Ephraim camp (108,100) + Dan camp (157,600) = 603,550.

**Verse 34 — "so they encamped...and so they set out":** The Hebrew uses a pair of כֵּן ("so/thus") clauses to confirm obedience to the divine command in both encampment and march. The LXX mirrors this with οὕτως...καὶ οὕτως.

**LXX Comparison — General:** The Vaticanus text of Numbers 2 closely follows the Hebrew in structure and content. The tribal names, leader names, and census figures are consistent between MT and LXX throughout this chapter. The primary differences are lexical (τάγμα for דֶּגֶל, σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου for אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד) rather than substantive.
